metadata.split_role: Expanded red/yellow, blue-staining, red-pored, and caution-lane bolete records.

review_pass_notes.rule: Clear stale image-only review only when at least two distinct real source-image URLs exist; keep one-photo and placeholder records in review.

records.culinary_uses: Some two-colored boletes are eaten, but this group is easy to confuse with faster-bluing or more suspect red/yellow boletes. Treat as experienced-level only.

mushroom_profile.summary: Minimal bolete seed entry for Bicolor Bolete.
staining: Pores bruise blue promptly; flesh usually faint/erratic blue
research_notes: Needs source links and fuller field notes.
stem_feature: Red stem with yellow near apex

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat unless true bicolor traits are confirmed: red cap/stem, bright yellow young pores, shallow tubes, and flesh that blues faintly or erratically rather than instantly everywhere.
records.field_identification: Red cap and mostly red stem, bright yellow pores that bruise blue, shallow tube layer, yellow flesh that usually stains blue only faintly or erratically, under hardwoods/oaks. Compare carefully with Boletus sensibilis and pseudosensibilis.

use_links.label: MushroomExpert: Boletus bicolor
use_links.notes: Red/yellow color pattern, shallow tubes, and blue bruising comparison.

records.edibility_notes: Some two-colored boletes are eaten, but this group is easy to confuse with faster-bluing or more suspect red/yellow boletes.

edible_use.notes: Cook confirmed young specimens; do not use if ID drifts toward fast-bluing or red/yellow caution boletes.

records.quality_notes: Good table value only when the bicolor bolete trait set is nailed down; not a beginner bolete.
records.eating_use_notes: Cook confirmed young specimens; do not use if ID drifts toward fast-bluing or red/yellow caution boletes.
records.risk_reason: Risk is confusion with Boletus sensibilis, B. pseudosensibilis, and other red/yellow blue-staining boletes.
records.hard_rule: Do not eat unless true bicolor traits are confirmed: red cap/stem, bright yellow young pores, shallow tubes, and flesh that blues faintly or erratically rather than instantly everywhere.

records.culinary_uses: Not a food target in this guide; retained for identification and caution comparison.

mushroom_profile.summary: Rare bicolor-like red/yellow bolete recorded from Michigan sources; treat as a specialist ID with low local confidence rather than a food target.

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat red/yellow blue-staining boletes from this record without specialist confirmation.
records.mushroom_card_note: Rare bicolor-like red/yellow bolete recorded from Michigan sources; treat as a specialist ID with low local confidence rather than a food target.
records.field_identification: Bicolor-like bolete with red/yellow colors and blue-staining flesh; key characters include long amyloid spores and a stem described as completely red in older treatments. Field ID should be treated as tentative without microscopy.
records.food_value: Not a food target in this guide
records.risk_reason: Red/yellow blue-staining bolete in a hard-to-separate caution group; field confidence is not high enough for food use.
records.quality_notes: Included for recognition, comparison, or caution rather than table value.
records.eating_use_notes: Do not use this app entry as a food recommendation.
records.hard_rule: Do not eat red/yellow blue-staining boletes from this record without specialist confirmation.

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat red/yellow blue-staining boletes from this record without specialist confirmation.
records.mushroom_card_note: Red-capped, blue-staining bolete in a hard-to-separate caution group; olive tones in the cap and red/yellow staining details matter.
records.field_identification: Small to medium red-capped bolete with yellow pores that blue and cap tones that can move toward olive. Compare with Boletus bicolor, sensibilis, pseudosensibilis, and other red-capped blue-stainers.

records.field_identification: Bright red cap, yellow pores that blue, yellow-and-red reticulate stem, and hardwood/oak association. Compare with bicolor, pseudopeckii, and other red/yellow boletes.

records.field_identification: Small bolete in the red/brown, blue-staining group. MushroomExpert keys it among smaller boletes with pore-surface and staining details; field name should remain tentative.

pore_color: Slot-like lines near stem in age

records.edibility_detail: Use only as ID/comparison in this app.
records.mushroom_card_note: Medium-small red-capped blue-stainer; slot-like pores near the stem help separate it from look-alikes.
records.field_identification: Dark red to brick-red cap fading with age, yellow pores that blue, pinkish-red stem with yellow apex/base, and pore surface that can develop slot-like/gill-like lines near the stem.

records.field_identification: Brownish red-pored bolete in the Boletus vermiculosus group. MushroomExpert treats subgraveolens under the vermiculosoides discussion; cap, odor, pore color, spores, and host matter.

mushroom_profile.summary: Brown-pored oak bolete that bruises blue/black; the whole vermiculosus group needs cautious, modern treatment.
cap_surface: Olive brown to medium brown cap
pore_color: Dark red-brown to maroon young pores

records.general_notes: Part of a difficult Brown-pored Bolete group rather than a simple duplicate. Treat species-level ID as tentative without expert review; compare cap color, pore color, staining, stem surface, and associated trees. This record represents lighter brown/yellowish cap tendency and smaller-spored concept reported in field-guide sources.
records.edibility_detail: Avoid for food; retained for ID/taxonomy comparison.
records.field_identification: Oak-associated bolete with dark reddish-brown to maroon young pores fading dull orange-brown, non-reticulate stem, and blue-to-black bruising. Often wormy and taxonomically tricky.
records.mushroom_card_note: Brown-pored oak bolete that bruises blue/black; the whole vermiculosus group needs cautious, modern treatment.
records.non_edible_severity: Caution / not a beginner food target
